title: Keeping consistency for man pages
body:
### Feature request description

Check man page PR's for consistency as defined [here.](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/MANPAGE_SYNTAX.md)

### Suggest potential solution

Create a bot to check these and inform the writer of the PR.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
What would the point of a bot? We already have a quite some man page test scripts that are run as checks on every PR.
So they could be extended to make even more syntax checks. 

But really there is the question how for we want to go. While I like consistency personally the fact is that each extra minor check makes contribution harder.
@mheon could you specify the placement of $ sign that we were hoping to avoid in the future?

We want every example that can be run as rootless prefixed by a `$` (we're only ~50% consistent on this). Root-only example commands (I don't think we have any of those, but I can see a world where we might...?) should be prefixed by `#`. And all examples should, when possible, include output. No output could be indicated by a trailing `$`? Just as an example

I agree with such style but enforcing that seems non trivial? One thing that exists are doc tests, rust has that concept for example. Basically each example would be execute as test and then you can cross reference the output. Of course the issue is that then you get into the problems of having to deal with "setup" commands that are needed before you can run the actual example command.

While that all sounds nice to have I am not sure if the complexity is worth the minor style problems.

I agree if it is complex it is not worth it. Let's see what AI might give us in the future for fixing after the fact. In these days I do think it is important that our documentation is consumed by machines clearly without inconsistencies. Feel free to close if it is too much work.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
